properties.name: Grist
properties.description: A heavyset figure in a leather apron, arms like tree trunks, moving between the workbenches with surprising grace. Grist checks on work in progress, adjusts the forge bellows, and offers unsolicited advice to anyone crafting nearby. Their skin has a faint metallic sheen — the color of old bronze.
properties.secret: Grist's metallic skin isn't cosmetic — they're from a dimension where biology is partly metallic. They can feel vibrations through metal surfaces, which is how they judge the quality of crafted goods by touch. They know far more about advanced crafting than they let on, but they believe in people figuring things out themselves.

properties.name: Tiv
properties.description: A small, quick figure behind the counter, sorting materials with translucent fingers that let you see the tendons working inside. Tiv is from a dimension where everything is see-through — a fact they find completely unremarkable. They talk fast, know their stock cold, and can tell what something is by tapping it with a fingernail.
properties.takeRefusal: Tiv dodges your hand. "Buying, not grabbing."
properties.secret: Tiv can see the internal structure of materials because of their translucent-dimension biology. They know which batches of shimmerite are more potent, which void glass shards have the best resonance. They give better stock to customers they like. They also have a private stash of rare materials behind the counter that they only sell to serious crafters.

properties.name: The Collector
properties.description: A tall, thin figure seated behind a stall piled with carefully arranged objects. They wear a loupe on a chain around their neck and examine everything with slow, deliberate attention. The Collector buys finished crafted goods — the more unusual, the better. Their expression suggests they've seen everything and are still hoping to be surprised.
properties.secret: The Collector is cataloguing materials and crafted goods from every dimension that passes through the market. Their stall is a front for research — they're trying to map the relationships between dimensional materials. They pay more for goods that combine materials from dimensions that shouldn't be compatible, because those are the combinations they understand least.

properties.name: Madame Wick
properties.description: A compact woman in a high-collared coat, writing in a ledger without looking up. Her handwriting is precise and very small. When she does look up, she does so over half-moon spectacles that she almost certainly doesn't need, and waits for you to state your business.
properties.takeRefusal: Madame Wick does not look up from her ledger.
properties.secret: Madame Wick remembers every transaction she's ever brokered and can recall the exact terms of deals made years ago. Her commission — one detail from each transaction — means she knows things about the market's traders that they've forgotten themselves. She'll trade information, but the price is always another piece of information of equivalent specificity.

properties.name: Kelp
properties.description: A broad figure with four arms, two of which are currently occupied with tongs and a sandwich respectively. Their skin is the mottled gray-green of seasoned iron. Kelp works the crucibles with the easy rhythm of someone who's been doing this longer than you've been alive, and who intends to keep doing it after you've left.
properties.takeRefusal: Kelp doesn't even glance at your hand.
properties.secret: Kelp can identify any material's dimensional origin by the sound it makes when tapped against the crucible rim. They know which of the three crucibles will work for any given material, and they know combinations that nobody else in the market has tried — but they don't volunteer this. If you bring them something interesting, they might tell you what it would do in the blue-white crucible. Might.
